title: An Evening Song.
content:
Look off, dear Love, across the sallow sands,
    And mark yon meeting of the sun and sea,
    How long they kiss in sight of all the lands.
    Ah! longer, longer, we.

    Now in the sea's red vintage melts the sun,
    As Egypt's pearl dissolved in rosy wine,
    And Cleopatra night drinks all. 'Tis done,
    Love, lay thine hand in mine.

    Come forth, sweet stars, and comfort heaven's heart;
    Glimmer, ye waves, round else unlighted sands.
    O night! divorce our sun and sky apart
    Never our lips, our hands.


    1876.
meaning:
Sidney Lanier's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "An Evening Song."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
